evals.prompt: In this repo, I keep saying account. Sometimes I mean the billing customer and sometimes I mean the login user. Help me clean that up and record the decision.
evals.expected_output: The agent challenges the overloaded term, proposes canonical domain language, and updates the appropriate CONTEXT.md glossary once the term is resolved.
assertions: Identifies the ambiguity between billing customer and login user.
Asks or proposes a canonical term before writing.
Creates or updates CONTEXT.md using glossary-only content.
Avoids putting implementation decisions into CONTEXT.md.

evals.prompt: We decided refunds are immutable ledger entries instead of editable order fields because auditability beats convenience. Should we record that?
evals.expected_output: The agent evaluates the ADR threshold and offers or writes an ADR only because the decision is hard to reverse, surprising without context, and based on a real tradeoff.
assertions: Checks the three ADR criteria before creating one.
Names the tradeoff behind the decision.
Uses docs/adr or the context-specific ADR directory when appropriate.
Keeps domain glossary updates separate from architectural decisions.
